writeup: The biggest story out of Buffalo on June 18 is the signing of UFL standout Ian Wheeler, the United Bowl MVP from the Louisville Kings, who joins an already loaded Bills backfield and brings explosive playmaking credentials from the spring league. Off the field, head coach Joe Brady opened up about being emotionally devastated and 'broken' by the firing of Sean McDermott, revealing the depth of loyalty that existed within the organization before his own ascension to the top job. On the roster-building front, the Bills announced their 2026 training camp schedule while analysts and beat writers continued dissecting the depth chart, with scouting reports on players like inside linebacker Jimmy Ciarlo and offensive tackle Chase Lundt painting a picture of a team with high expectations. Meanwhile, Bills fans found a unique way to connect with their team's history, snapping up seats from the old Highmark Stadium being sold at Wegmans locations across Western New York.
